message:
Migrate TUI layer from ratatui/crossterm to FrankenTUI native stack

Why: The TUI was built on ratatui + crossterm, which worked but carried
several pain points:

  1. Terminal setup/teardown required manual raw-mode, alternate-screen,
     and mouse-capture management with explicit cleanup on every exit path.
  2. The crossterm event loop used synchronous polling with manual timeout
     arithmetic, making async integration fragile.
  3. Test rendering relied on ratatui's TestBackend which coupled snapshot
     tests to ratatui's internal buffer layout.

FrankenTUI (ftui) resolves all three:

  - TtyBackend uses RAII (Drop) for terminal cleanup, eliminating the
    manual restore-on-error pattern in app.rs.
  - The ftui event model integrates natively with async runtimes,
    simplifying the event loop in event.rs.
  - ftui's Frame + Buffer + GraphemePool API gives direct control over
    rendering without coupling to a specific backend, making test_harness
    snapshots backend-agnostic.

What changed:
  - app.rs: Replace enable_raw_mode/execute!/LeaveAlternateScreen with
    TtyBackend::open() and BackendFeatures/TtySessionOptions. Terminal
    cleanup is now automatic via Drop.
  - event.rs: Rewrite event polling to use ftui-backend's
    BackendEventSource trait instead of crossterm::event::poll/read.
  - mod.rs: Update module-level re-exports for the new ftui types.
  - widgets.rs: Port all widget rendering from ratatui primitives
    (Paragraph, Block, Borders, Table, Row, Cell, Gauge, Tabs, etc.)
    to their ftui-widgets equivalents. Style/color constants migrated
    from ratatui::style to ftui-style.
  - test_harness.rs: Replace TestBackend/Terminal with Frame + Buffer +
    GraphemePool for snapshot rendering. Update buffer-to-string
    extraction to use ftui's cell API.

No behavioral changes — the TUI looks and behaves identically to users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
